perturbation.original_text: 5.5.5 All invoices delivered in accordance with Section 5.5.2 or 5.5.4 shall be paid by the Paying Party within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5.5 All invoices delivered in accordance with Section 5.5.2 or 5.5.4 shall be paid by the Paying Party within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of such invoice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Janssen shall have 120 days to remit any invoice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that gives Janssen a specific payment timeline of 120 days to remit invoices, which conflicts with the original clause that requires all invoices to be paid within [***] days after receipt. This perturbation leads to uncertainty regarding which payment timeline should be followed for Janssen, which could cause disputes over late fees or breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5.5 All invoices delivered in accordance with Section 5.5.2 or 5.5.4 shall be paid by the Paying Party within [***] ([***]) days after receipt of such invoice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Janssen shall have 120 days to remit any invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by specifying a different payment timeline (120 days for Janssen) than the general timeline ([***] days) stated for all invoices in both sections 5.5.5. This directly contradicts the original requirement for all invoices delivered under 5.5.2 or 5.5.4, creating a scenario where it's unclear which payment term applies to Janssen, thus meeting the definition of inconsistencies leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14.7 Amendments; Assignment. This Agreement may not be revised, amended, supplemented, or varied except by an instrument in writing signed by Janssen and Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.7 Amendments; Assignment. This Agreement may not be revised, amended, supplemented, or varied except by an instrument in writing signed by Janssen. Company’s consent to any amendment is not required.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the contract can only be amended with a signed instrument by Janssen, and explicitly stating that company consent is not required. This directly conflicts with the beginning of the clause, which states any changes to the agreement require a signed instrument by Janssen and Company, creating uncertainty about the amendment process and enforcement, since any change could be made by Janssen without consent from the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.7 Amendments; Assignment. This Agreement may not be revised, amended, supplemented, or varied except by an instrument in writing signed by Janssen. Company’s consent to any amendment is not required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 14.7 to state that 'Company’s consent to any amendment is not required' while the original text and contradicted text states that amendments require 'an instrument in writing signed by Janssen and Company.' This creates a direct contradiction as the modified text allows Janssen to unilaterally amend the agreement, conflicting with the requirement of both parties' consent.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1.1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed in writing, each of Janssen and Company agrees that, during the Term and until the conclusion of the [***] ([***]) year period beginning upon the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, such Party shall: (a) maintain in confidence the Confidential Information of the other Party using not less than the efforts such Party uses to maintain in confidence its own confidential or proprietary information of similar kind and value (but not less than reasonable efforts); (b) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other Party to any Third Party; and (c) not use the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1.1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed in writing, each of Janssen and Company agrees that, during the Term and until the conclusion of the [***] ([***]) year period beginning upon the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, such Party shall: (a) maintain in confidence the Confidential Information of the other Party using not less than reasonable efforts; (b) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other Party to any Third Party; and (c) not use the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement. Notwithstanding, Janssen may disclose confidential information to Third Parties as necessary.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that allows Janssen to disclose confidential information to third parties as necessary, which directly conflicts with the original non-disclosure clause prohibiting disclosure of confidential information to any third party. This creates legal uncertainty as to whether Janssen is truly bound by the confidentiality provisions, and also on how confidentiality is being regarded in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1.1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed in writing, each of Janssen and Company agrees that, during the Term and until the conclusion of the [***] ([***]) year period beginning upon the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, such Party shall: (a) maintain in confidence the Confidential Information of the other Party using not less than reasonable efforts; (b) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other Party to any Third Party; and (c) not use the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement. Notwithstanding, Janssen may disclose confidential information to Third Parties as necessary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and wording of the non-disclosure agreement. The addition of "Notwithstanding, Janssen may disclose confidential information to Third Parties as necessary" allows Janssen to disclose confidential information, which directly conflicts with the original clause 9.1.1 (b) which states that neither party shall "not disclose the Confidential Information of the other Party to any Third Party". This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding confidentiality obligations.
